TextENGNew: I had hoped Satoko would be there when I woke that morning.
I had hoped this incomprehensible world would turn out to be nothing more than a groggy dream.
So when I opened my eyes to the ceiling of the Furude main house instead of the familiar one I knew... I felt deflated.
As my hazy, just-woken mind cleared, the reality of my situation came flooding back...
Through my own carelessness, I had stumbled into this strange world.
Hanyuu's power could not reach here, making it extraordinarily difficult to escape.
...It was like trying to climb a spider's thread all the way to paradise.
But the spider's thread was still dangling.
No matter how slender it was... there was still a chance.
I needed to find whatever was blocking Hanyuu.
...And I didn't even know what form it might take.
The only clue, if you could call it that, was that since Hanyuu was Hinamizawa's deity, it was probably somewhere in Hinamizawa.
I picked up the crystal ball I had taken from the ritual storehouse.
...The ball held a faint glow, different from natural light.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: When I pressed it to my forehead... I could detect a subtle presence, perceptible only to a witch like me.
Whatever I needed to find would almost certainly radiate the same kind of presence.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: But like this crystal ball, it would probably be something I could only faintly sense by pressing it against my forehead.
...It didn't seem like I could just wander around and pick up the signal.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Still, if the object was related to Hanyuu's power like this crystal ball, it was most likely inside the ritual storehouse.
Unfortunately, the storehouse was a chaotic jumble of ancient torture implements, mountains of old documents, and heaps of unidentifiable junk.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: The only remotely tidy areas were around the sacred object and the altar.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Searching through all of that for something potentially as small as this crystal ball would be no simple task.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Rika!``@k@vS15/00/DS42220007. Finish your breakfast and get to school!``
My mother's voice came from the kitchen.
...The school in this world was not the school I knew.
If I had time to waste at a place like that, I should be searching the ritual storehouse instead.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: But there was no guarantee my search would be over in a day or two.
...As much as I hated to admit it... given that I didn't even know what I was looking for... I might need to steel myself for a search that could take years.
If I skipped school, Chie -- ever the diligent teacher -- would surely call the house to ask about my absence.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...That would create complications with both the school and my parents.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: I couldn't even begin to guess how long this would take.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: If my parents grew suspicious of me, everything would become harder.
...Haste makes waste, as they say.
changeReason: Perspective shift from first-person to third-person narration.

TextENGNew: ...Frustrating as it was, I would be better off obediently attending school on weekdays.
I let out a weary sigh, dragged myself up, and went to eat breakfast.
Throughout the entire meal, my mother kept lecturing me about never entering the ritual storehouse again, thoroughly ruining the taste of my food.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...My mother was relentless and long-winded.
I despised that about her.
The village elders laughed and said it was because she doted on her only daughter so much she couldn't help herself, but to me it was nothing but a nuisance.
I didn't need their explanations about tough love.
The only thing I could be grateful for was having a meal prepared without doing it myself.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ......If anything, I'd rather make my own meals if it meant she'd leave me alone.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Even as I headed out for school, my mother peppered my back with nagging about not being late.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Ahh, how irritating...
If it meant not seeing my mother's face for half the day, maybe going to school wasn't so bad.
I said my thanks for the meal, dropped my dishes in the sink, and left for school without looking back.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: If this world was going to defy my expectations at every turn in such a nauseating way, it might as well defy them with the school lessons too.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ...But even in this world, the curriculum progressed exactly as it did in the world I knew.
Repeating a world I thought I had already escaped.
...That thought alone made the tedium even heavier.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Normally I would distract myself by teasing Satoko next to me, but in this world my seat was far from hers.
She must have found class just as tedious.
She kept poking at Satoshi, who sat beside her studying earnestly.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Mion also seemed to be pestering Reina.
But rather than horsing around, they were helping each other study -- a strange but heartwarming sight.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Actually, it wasn't just them; the whole classroom had that warm atmosphere.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: I was the only one left out of it.

I didn't know whether my desk had been placed in its own row at the back due to an odd number of students,@k or whether this world's version of me had deliberately chosen this seat -- but either way, my desk alone felt severed from the classroom.
changeReason: New translation omits content present in the original.

TextENGNew: That sense of isolation became even sharper at lunchtime.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: My classmates split into groups of friends and opened their lunches.
The club members who always ate with me didn't exist here.
...Satoko sat in a circle with several friends, her brother among them.
There was no opening for me to squeeze into.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Mion and Reina sat across from each other, eating together.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...No room for me there, either.
And no one invited me to eat with them.
They all ate happily, as if this were perfectly normal -- as if it had always been this way.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: For a while, I sat and waited, hoping Satoko or Mion or Reina would call me over.
...But as five minutes passed, then ten, I realized I'd miss lunch entirely if I kept waiting.@k ...So I finally gave up and opened my lunch box.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Inside were leftover charred hamburg steak from last night, and rice smothered in soggy nori.
Spinach wrapped in limp, mushy nori.
And the remains of some kinpira gobo, also burned black.
...Oh, right. I'd forgotten about that.
For whatever reason, my mother had a habit of overcooking everything.
Her mantra was that food must be "thoroughly heated in summer to be safe," so she'd char hamburg steak until it was rock-hard, and her grilled fish was always half carbon.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Even Satoko handled heat better than that.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...Ahh, so that was why -- when I learned to cook from Hanyuu and started making food that tasted better than my mother's, she must have been displeased.
The taste was awful.
Not a hint of color or presentation.
......If Mion ever proposed a lunchbox contest with this as my entry, last place would be guaranteed.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: But... there was no club in this world, so I didn't need to worry about that...
Hearing Satoko's happy laughter -- and knowing it wasn't directed at me -- made me sad.
...Just then, my eyes happened to meet Satoko's.
changeReason: New translation adds specific name/reference: satoko.

TextENGNew: Did my feelings... reach her...?
Satoko rose sharply from her seat and fixed me with a harsh glare.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: ``...You've been staring at me this whole time. What do you want?``@k@vS15/04/DS42040016. If you've got something to say, just say it.``
``I... I don't have anything to say.``
``Cut it out, Satoko.``@k@vS15/07/DS42210014. Your eyes just happened to meet. That's all.``
``It wasn't just 'happening'!``@k@vS15/04/DS42040018. She's been staring at me this whole time!``@k@vS15/04/DS42040019. It's so creepy!``
Satoko and her friends huddled together, whispering among themselves while glancing my way.
I couldn't make out the words, but it was easy to imagine they were the kind I was better off not hearing...
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...I'm sorry.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050190. I won't stare anymore.``
``You'd better not!``@k@vS15/04/DS42040021. You're ruining the taste of my cream croquettes!``
I tilted my desk slightly so I could no longer see Satoko and the others.
...At first I had called this world's Satoko 'cold,' but it seemed that wasn't quite right.
This world's Satoko didn't simply disregard me -- she actively disliked me. Perhaps even hated me.
And the children close to my age all seemed to orbit around Satoko.@k ...There was no place for me in their group, and... everyone seemed to dislike me.
changeReason: New translation adds specific name/reference: satoko.

TextENGNew: ...The charcoal-hard steak tasted like salt...
Why... was I eating such a lonely, sad meal...?
In my real world... I would be eating lunch happily with all the club members.
Satoko would be there, and Rena, and Mion, and Keiichi... We'd all be laughing and chatting, enjoying each other's company even more than the food...
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Just then, Reina walked over and peeked into my lunch box.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Your lunch is hamburg steak and spinach today, Rika-chan?``@k@vS15/02/DS42020056. That looks delicious.``
It was painfully obvious that Reina had come to comfort me in my misery... and for some inexplicable reason, that only made me feel more bitter. I didn't want to respond.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: When I said nothing, she held out a little octopus-shaped sausage right in front of my face.
``Want to trade? My octopus for your spinach roll.``@k@vS15/02/DS42020058. How about it?``
changeReason: New translation adds substantial additional content.

TextENGNew: Reina was trying to be kind to me -- me, eating alone, rejected by everyone. ...And yet I couldn't bring myself to accept it.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: I abruptly announced I was going to the bathroom, walked away... and in a stall, pressing toilet paper to my face, I cried.
...The paper dissolved into a soggy mess from my tears, but I didn't care...
...It was fine.
It didn't matter if I had no friends in this world.
In the world I needed to return to, I had plenty of friends.
There, Satoko was my dearest, irreplaceable best friend... and the club members who would never leave me alone at lunch were all waiting for me.
...So being treated coldly by some Satoko I didn't know, in a world I didn't belong to -- that shouldn't make me sad.
It shouldn't make me sad...
And yet... no matter what lies I told myself, I couldn't stop the tears for a long while...
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: ...I froze my heart over and decided to simply wait for school to end.
Then I'd rush home, tell them I was going out to play, and sneak into the ritual storehouse.
...That was the only way back to my original world.
I would focus everything on that one goal.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ......All right.
I peered into the bathroom mirror. A face swollen from crying stared back.
...If Chie saw me like this, it would only complicate things further.
I washed my face, then spent the rest of lunch break tucked away in a corner of the classroom.
When school let out, I bolted from the classroom.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: I got caught up in the flood of classmates all rushing to go play, which was incredibly annoying.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: I thought Satoshi called out to me, but I ignored him.
...He was probably just going to apologize on his sister's behalf for the lunchtime incident.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: I had no interest in that.
I didn't want to remain in this sickening world a single second longer than necessary.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: I sprinted home at full speed, ignored my mother's usual question about homework, and searched for a flashlight.
Then I announced I was going out to play and dashed out the door.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: I circled the shrine grounds, looking for my father.
A sign reading 'Shrine Committee Meeting' hung on the assembly hall door.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...My father's voice mingled with the laughter and chatter inside.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...Just like yesterday, this was my chance.
Today I'd have to be more careful not to give away my presence inside.
After confirming no one was watching, I nimbly climbed the tree I had mastered the day before.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Yesterday the height had frightened me, but after crying my heart out at lunch and steeling my resolve, something that trivial no longer scared me.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...What I truly needed to fear was not heights, nor my parents, nor this cold Satoko.
...It was the possibility of never returning to my original world.
The next most frightening thing... might be losing contact with Hanyuu.
In the darkness of the storehouse, I drew the crystal ball from my pocket.
...Its feeble glow seemed slightly more vigorous than the day before.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Perhaps rest allowed a little of her power to recover.
Even so, it was still faint.
...If I squandered it on idle conversation, the light might vanish entirely.
I firmly suppressed the urge to confirm that communication was still possible and switched on my flashlight.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ...Time to begin the treasure hunt.
Was there anything here -- anything only I could perceive?
...The same kind of presence as this crystal ball... somewhere...
Clinging to that hope, I closed my eyes and sharpened all five senses.
...But the only sound that reached my ears was the droning of cicadas.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: As expected, this wasn't going to be easy.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...Too optimistic, I suppose.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050195. ...Sigh......``
How long would it take to search this entire storehouse from top to bottom?
...I didn't want to think about it.
...But I had to do it, or I could never go home.
...I would approach this methodically.
Start in one corner, work my way across.
Not knowing what the object looked like, I would go corner by corner, leaving no stone unturned.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: And yet, there was no guarantee it was even in here.
......Fighting Takano and the Mountain Dogs all over again would honestly be easier than this...
``...I don't think it's gotten any weaker.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070105. Your voice sounds about the same volume as before.``
``Meaning it still only reaches you faintly, as always.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050197. ...We shouldn't waste time on idle chatter.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...No, I think a little conversation is exactly what you need right now, Rika.``

``.........Maybe.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050199. Thank you.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050200. ......I'll talk, then.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050201. ...I've been searching for about a week now, but I still don't even know what I'm looking for.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ...I searched for about a week. I still don't know what it looks like. Is there really something here...?
``Honestly... this is brutal.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050206. ......If only I knew what it looked like.``
changeReason: New translation omits content present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``I understand.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070109. I know this is an incredibly difficult search...``@k@vS15/12/DS42070110. But I've been thinking about it too, and I may have learned something.``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``And?``@k@vS15/05/DS42050210. Did you find anything out?``
``...Rika, in your world, Fragments are perceived as 'feelings.'``@k@vS15/12/DS42070112. But in your current world, where Fragments don't exist as a concept, they might have taken a different form.``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I follow the logic... but that doesn't give me much of a lead.``
``...Au.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070114. ...As for how to return the Fragment to this world and restore it... you need to completely destroy whatever is containing it.``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Destroy it?``@k@vS15/05/DS42050213. I have to break it?``
``Yes, completely.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070116. Once it's lost from the human world, the Fragment will be released back into its original realm.``
``...Are you familiar with the law of conservation of matter?``@k@vS15/05/DS42050215. Matter can change form, but it never disappears. So how do I get rid of it from the human world?``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``That's why I said you only need to destroy its form.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070118. It won't vanish from the world -- it will simply change into something else.``
changeReason: New translation removes specific name/reference: rika.

TextENGNew: ``.........I see. All right.``
The way ancients sent offerings to the gods.
A way to remove something from the human world...
``You're telling me to burn it.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050218. Burn it to ash and send it up to the gods as smoke.``
``That's right.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070121. By burning something to ashes, it loses its original form, and from a human perspective, it ceases to exist.``
``...I see.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050220. So I need to find whatever's housing the Fragment and burn it.``
changeReason: New translation omits content present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``......Actually, Rika...``@k@vS15/12/DS42070123. The Fragment might not only be contained in an object.``
``I see -- because Fragments are 'feelings.'``@k@vS15/05/DS42050224. They might not be housed in objects alone, but in people as well.``
changeReason: New translation removes specific name/reference: hanyuu.

TextENGNew: ``......A human's meaning ceases the moment they lose their life.``
``That would make sense.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050228. Corpses aren't people anymore.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050229. If a Fragment is housed in a person... killing that person would release it.``
......Until now I had assumed I was searching for an object, but it might just as easily be a person.
changeReason: Significantly different wording changes the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: I'd have no qualms about burning an object.
...But if it turned out to be not an object, but a person...
``............I don't like where this is going.``@k@vS15/05/DS42050231. ......This world... something about it seems designed to test me.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...I can only pray it doesn't come to that.``
``If it's always the worst possibilities that prove true, then I can use that to my advantage... I'd rather not, though.``
changeReason: New translation removes specific name/reference: oyashiro.

TextENGNew: ``Yes.``@k@vS15/12/DS42070128. Most likely...``
``...Is it possible it's me?``
``No, it isn't.@k@vS15/12/DS42070130. If you were the Fragment rejecting me, you wouldn't be able to talk to me right now.``

``...Which means...... next is my father, head priest of the Furude Shrine... and my mother, carrier of the Furude lineage.@k@vS15/05/DS42050236. ......If it was one of them, I'd just have to kill them, right?``
My human morality refused the idea of killing my parents.
...But that was nothing a world-crossing witch needed to think about.
For someone with a true world somewhere else, this was a false world.
...Meaning it was the same as a dream.
Murdering someone in a dream was no different from doing it in a delusion or daydream.
There was nothing to consider about committing the sin in this world...
Hanyuu was making sad ``au au`` noises, so I told her how I felt.
``...Stop being such a hypocrite.@k@vS15/05/DS42050238. ...Would you rather give up on returning to our original world?``

``You're a coward.@k@vS15/05/DS42050240. You're going to force me to dirty my hands, while never dirtying yours.@k@vS15/05/DS42050241. ...Convenient, isn't it? If you can blame it all on one person, nobody else will be marked with sin.@k@vS15/05/DS42050242. I'm the perfect human sacrifice.``
I felt those words were a little harsh on Hanyuu.
For a short while, she remained silent, not saying anything.
...But her breathing made it sound like she wanted to say something, but was hesitating.

``...I... want to go back to the final world.@k@vS15/05/DS42050244. ......Rena said this once.@k@vS15/05/DS42050245. 'I wonder how much we're allowed to struggle in order to be happy...'@k@vS15/05/DS42050246. I didn't think there was a limit to that effort.``
But Keiichi changed that.
There's no limit to effort.
...But you have to talk to a lot of people to get their advice on whether your efforts are misplaced or not.
That's why I was talking about it with Hanyuu, the only one who shared my current situation.
......And Hanyuu said that if the object possessing the Fragment was a person, I had to kill them.
...I would want to avoid that, of course, if there were any other way.
But if she said that was the only way...... then I wouldn't hesitate.
That was all.
``.........I... I want to go back too...``
``I'll be praying... that the Fragment... isn't held by a person.``
``Please, do.@k@vS15/05/DS42050250. ...I will too.@k@vS15/05/DS42050251. ......And I apologize.@k@vS15/05/DS42050252. I was too hard on you.

...All you can do is look for clues and keep my spirits up so I don't get discouraged.@k@vS15/05/DS42050254. ...I didn't understand how annoying it must be not to be able to look for the key yourself.``
``......It isn't as hard as it is for you, Rika.@k@vS15/12/DS42070137. ...The only thing I can do... is at least be here to care for you, so that your heart doesn't tire out...``
``...Thanks.@k@vS15/05/DS42050256. These idle chats with you are the most enjoyable thing about this world.@k@vS15/05/DS42050257. It's like I wake up from the nightmare for a few minutes.``
...I see.
I sort of understood why she said chatting could be important.
Hanyuu was the only one I could talk to from my original world.
If I lose contact with Hanyuu... maybe my very connection to that world would weaken, and my memories would blur.
And that... would simply give birth to a miserable, broken-hearted girl, living a delusion that this world wasn't the one she belonged in.

``...Leave it to me.@k@vS15/05/DS42050259. I swear we'll go back there together.@k@vS15/05/DS42050260. ...You keep looking around for any clues you can find.@k@vS15/05/DS42050261. ...If there are any developments on my end, I'll contact you again.``
``......I understand.@k@vS15/12/DS42070140. ......Rika, hold it together.@k@vS15/12/DS42070141. ......Go for it, yay. Remember?``
I couldn't hear Hanyuu anymore.
The crystal ball's light had grown weaker than ever.
...But as long as it had its otherworldly glow... then I could keep believing this wasn't the world I was supposed to live in.
Before, I might have shed a tear or two by now.
But all the tears I could cry had dried up.
I gripped the crystal ball tightly, savoring the cheer Hanyuu had given me.
......Do your best.
...Go for it, yay...
...I moved over to a spot with a pile of old documents.
If it could interfere with Hanyuu, it had to be related to the legends of Oyashiro-sama.
...So maybe, I thought, a clue would be written in one of the documents in that pile.
Maybe even if the thing I searched for wasn't in here, I could find a clue to it instead.
I took a few documents from the pile and packed them into the knapsack I brought with me.
I could read some, but couldn't understand any of the others.
...If something important was in one of the indecipherable ones, I would have no way of knowing.
But I shouldn't think about that right now.
I pulled out the few documents I could read and decided to read them in secret at school and in bed.
As I went in and out of the storehouse, the chances of my parents finding out increased.
...In that sense, this would lower that risk, which made it a little easier for me.
......Maybe parents did understand their children best, and would be the first ones to help them...@k but that was when they were normal, and not a witch like me.
If their daughter started babbling about not being from this world, they'd probably tie her down and summon a noisy ambulance.
...If I were a little more cunning, I could flatter my parents and put the work in to make this world a little more bearable.
But the more I thought about this world not being the one I lived in, the more apathetic I felt towards interacting with those who were here.
That was probably why it seemed like I was being more and more isolated in class.
...Of course, maybe you could say that was my own fault.
That was the kind of world this was at the moment ``I`` arrived; I won't dare let anyone say that this is my fault.
Of course, not a single person could tell the difference between Rika Furude and ``me`` anyway.
The ``me`` that isn't Rika Furude... Who is she?
I came around to a quiet, electronic beep.
I looked at my watch and noticed it was already evening.
Being in here made me lose track of time.
The other day I was out way past curfew, and was scolded for a long time; the alarm on my watch was to prevent that.
...Letting my relationship with my parents grow any worse would make everything else more troublesome.
...I should call it quits for today.
With practiced motions, I climbed the bundle of chains and headed through the exit in the roof.
When I returned to the main house, surrounded by the cries of higurashi that I'd grown completely tired of,@k there was a tightly packed case of beer near the front door... and three bottles of wine.
The beer case was probably for a drinking party at the shrine's assembly hall or something.
...My father didn't drink beer because he liked it, so I knew it had to be for the assembly hall.
They delivered it here before the party so he could chill it beforehand in the assembly hall refrigerator.
And... The wine probably belonged to my father.
My father enjoys Western spirits, and wine most of all. ...And wine, at the same time, was something I enjoyed as well.
Before, after my parents had died in 1981,
I'd gotten wine by bringing it from the main house,
but my parents were alive in this world,
so I couldn't do that.
I stared at the bottles longingly as I tried to open the door, receiving the clack of its lock in response.
I took a key from my pocket, unlocked the door, went in, and saw a note addressed to me right upon entering.
It was from my mother.
The note said an accident had suddenly occurred at the Kimiyoshi house, and they'd gone out to help with a memorial service.
They'd be late coming back, and so on and so forth.
Written as a postscript was a notice that the brewer would be bringing alcohol, and to take it inside the front door once I got back for them.
...I see. That must have been referring to all of this.
If the person had come while I was here, I could have told them to put it inside... but I couldn't bear to see them outside like this, put here before I got back.
And it would be a pain if my mother started rambling about me being inconsiderate later.
...I made a sour face, not caring who saw, and pulled the case of beer through the front door.
...Next, I stood before the wine bottles... and solidified my thoughts.
Couldn't there have been a slip-up with the order?
......It was a brewery in a small village.
If my father called to say he'd ordered one more bottle, they'd probably bring over another one without a second thought.
...So what would it matter if one of these disappeared......?
I always dilute it before drinking, so I'd be able to enjoy one bottle of wine for a pretty long time.
Despite being in the entrance, I belatedly looked around, grabbed a bottle, ran into my room,
and then pushed it into the winter futon in my closet that I wouldn't use for a while.
Actually... My parents live here too, so I won't have many chances to drink...
I ran to the kitchen, grabbed a glass, ice, a corkscrew... and a carton of orange juice, then ran back into my room.
...I put the corkscrew on the wine bottle,
and was overwhelmed by the urge to wet my tongue
with the nostalgic flavor as soon as I could.
How was I supposed to survive in this world without enjoying a good wine?
I poured some into the glass, and just listening to the orange juice splashing in to fill it up made my mouth water like a dog.
...If I dilute it this much, it'll look just like orange juice from the outside.
But...... it was the nostalgic flavor that belonged to my world.
At one time, this had been the flavor of my tears of defeat, shed for the unbreakable June of 1983.
...But now, even those tears of defeat felt sweet and tender...
Those dead-end worlds were very painful.
But I was still... surrounded by my kind friends.
I had the leeway to choose to surrender to Takano and continue lazily living in that infinite loop.
...But even that warmth was absent in this world.
......This made a week now, since I came here.
With no prospects of returning to my previous world, I'd resolved myself to staying here for a long time—but a week in, it had already driven me to drink.
It'll be fine...
Those worlds had driven me to the same state.
...And I still managed, lazily doing my best as I soaked in wine, right?
...I needed to be released from this Rika Furude quickly.
Nobody in this world would understand that, even if I explained it.
That meant I had no allies here.
If I told someone... they'd think I'd gone crazy.
But considering this world's Rika Furude, that was only natural.
...This world's Rika Furude was a dour girl without a single friend.
And one day, she'd been hit by a ball in the schoolyard, and started saying she didn't belong in this world.
...If that big doctor, Yamamoto, heard about this, he'd probably make a huge fuss about the impact having caused brain damage.
But... that would make the most sense to the people who live here.
At the very least, until ``I`` arrived, Rika Furude was Rika Furude, the correct being for this world.
Maybe it was more appropriate to say that one day, ``I,`` someone different from this world's Rika, appeared, then took over Rika's body.
...Am I... not... Rika Furude?
Well, no.
This world's Rika Furude was this world's Rika Furude, a different being than me.
I'd simply been interpreting her as being the same as me, even when I was in different worlds—until now.
...When I thought about that... leaving aside whether I'd been living happily......@k I felt like I'd done something bad to this world's Rika Furude.
Of course, it was too late to apologize now, and I couldn't give this body back to her anymore.
...If there was a way to apologize, it would be to acknowledge this world's Rika Furude as a being of her own.
Because if I called myself Rika Furude, the girl who already existed would vanish from this life...
Which meant the only way to apologize was... to not call myself... Rika Furude.
...Well, that had been bothering me for a long time.
I've long felt that my young self, pampered as the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama, and my old self, who had awakened and traveled countless worlds, had different personalities.
But if I thought of them not as personalities, but as different beings entirely...
Right now, I was... no longer Rika Furude.
I...... Who am I?
I stared vacantly at the wine bottle.
Did I like this brand? My father always loved to drink this wine.
...As a result, I'd borrowed it in countless worlds, and I loved to drink it even now.
Right now, I couldn't even be lazy without borrowing the power of alcohol, and this wine comforted me, just a little.
...At this point, I couldn't go on living without this wine.
So, in other words, I wasn't alive, but the wine was...
Which meant I wasn't Rika Furude... and that I was the wine...
...I stared at the Western letters of the brand name written on the wine label, which I didn't know how to pronounce... and thought to myself that it was the name of ``me.``
My name is Rika Furude...@k no, not that... but this ``Bernkastel.``
...I don't know what that means.
Maybe I'll ask my father.
@bFuruderika.@<Frederica@> Bernkastel.
...Ah, whatever. What a silly name that is.
Aha ha ha.
...My thoughts began to slur and muddle.
Cheers... to this shithole of a world.
